$comment: UI node-type catalog. Served by the agent via list_node_types. When you add a new node component, append an entry here so the agent can compose with it.

$handle_convention.note: Every React Flow edge handle ID has the shape '<kind>:<name>'. 'param:<name>' is an event/value port; 'stream:<name>' is a frame/audio port. Example valid handle IDs: 'param:value', 'param:noise_scale', 'param:__prompt', 'param:__vace', 'stream:video', 'stream:vace_input_frames'. Do NOT use 'parameter:<name>' — it is invalid.

aggregate_pipeline_handles.param:__prompt: Pipeline prompt aggregate — connect from any string-valued output (primitive value, control value, prompt_blend prompts, subgraph prompt output). One wire replaces the built-in prompt text field.
aggregate_pipeline_handles.param:__vace: Pipeline VACE aggregate — connect from a 'vace' node's 'param:__vace' output. Pipeline must have supports_vace=true (check get_pipeline_handles).
aggregate_pipeline_handles.param:__loras: Pipeline LoRA aggregate — connect from a 'lora' node's 'param:lora' output. Pipeline must have supports_lora=true.

pipeline_stream_inputs: stream:video — required frame input on every compute pipeline
$handle_convention.subgraph_rule: External edges reference a subgraph via the NAMES declared in 'data.subgraphInputs[].name' / 'data.subgraphOutputs[].name'. The edge handle is 'param:<that name>'. Internal wiring lives in 'data.subgraphNodes' + 'data.subgraphEdges'.
$handle_convention.authoritative_source: frontend/src/lib/graphUtils.ts -> buildHandleId(). When in doubt call get_pipeline_handles(pipeline_id) for an exact list of targetable handles on a given pipeline.

node_types.label: Source
node_types.description: External input (camera, video file, Spout, NDI, Syphon).

node_types.label: Pipeline
node_types.description: A generative or transform pipeline. Input parameter handles depend on the pipeline schema — always call get_pipeline_handles(pipeline_id) before wiring edges to a pipeline. The answer from that tool is authoritative.

node_types.label: Sink
node_types.description: Output — WebRTC preview by default; set sink_mode for Spout/NDI/Syphon output.

node_types.label: Record
node_types.description: Records the input stream to an MP4 file. Controlled via start_recording / stop_recording.

node_types.label: Primitive
node_types.description: Static value producer — strings (prompts), numbers, booleans.

node_types.label: Slider
node_types.description: Continuous float control with min/max.

node_types.label: Knobs
node_types.description: Bank of rotary knobs (multi-value).

node_types.label: XY Pad
node_types.description: 2D positional control.

node_types.label: Bool
node_types.description: Toggle.

node_types.label: Trigger
node_types.description: Momentary button — fires a pulse. Wire its 'param:value' into a subgraph input (e.g. 'param:trigger_a') or to a control node's 'param:item_N' switch input.

node_types.label: Control
node_types.description: Generic controller. In 'switch' mode (data.controlMode = 'switch') it selects among variadic 'param:item_N' inputs (numeric triggers) and emits the corresponding 'param:str_N' string slot on its 'param:value' output. Use this to build prompt-switcher subgraphs.

inputs.name: str_N
inputs.note: In switch mode only — param:str_0, param:str_1, ... paired with item_N

node_types.label: Math
node_types.description: Arithmetic ops on numeric inputs.

node_types.label: Tuple
node_types.description: Group several values into a structured bundle.

node_types.label: Reroute
node_types.description: Visual re-routing point — passes value through.

node_types.label: Note
node_types.description: Freeform annotation — not executable.

node_types.label: Image
node_types.description: Static image reference (local file path). Wire 'param:value' into a 'vace' node's 'param:ref_image' (or 'param:first_frame' / 'param:last_frame').

node_types.label: Audio
node_types.description: Audio input/output.

node_types.label: VACE
node_types.description: VACE reference-image conditioning node. Takes up to three image paths + an optional context_scale modulator, and emits a single aggregate 'param:__vace' output that plugs into a pipeline's 'param:__vace' input (pipeline must have supports_vace=true). Typical wiring: image -> vace.ref_image, then vace.__vace -> pipeline.__vace.

inputs.name: ref_image
inputs.note: File path for reference image. Wire from an 'image' node's 'param:value'.

inputs.name: context_scale
inputs.note: Modulate 0..2 via a slider if desired; defaults to the node's internal value.

outputs.name: __vace
outputs.note: Aggregate output — connect ONLY to a pipeline's 'param:__vace' input.

node_types.label: LoRA
node_types.description: LoRA adapter — attaches to a pipeline, sets scale. Wire 'param:lora' into a pipeline's 'param:__loras' (pipeline must have supports_lora=true).

outputs.name: lora
outputs.note: Aggregate output — connect ONLY to a pipeline's 'param:__loras' input.

node_types.label: MIDI
node_types.description: MIDI input (CC / note / clock).

node_types.label: Tempo
node_types.description: Ableton Link / MIDI-clock tempo source.

node_types.label: Subgraph
node_types.description: Nested graph container. Internal nodes live in 'data.subgraphNodes' and internal edges in 'data.subgraphEdges'. The subgraph exposes external ports via 'data.subgraphInputs' and 'data.subgraphOutputs' — each entry has {name, portType: 'param'|'stream', paramType, innerNodeId, innerHandleId}. External edges from/to this subgraph use 'param:<subgraphInputs[].name>' or 'param:<subgraphOutputs[].name>' as the edge handle ID.

structure.subgraphNodes: Array of internal UI nodes (same shape as top-level ui_state.nodes).
structure.subgraphEdges: Array of internal edges between subgraphNodes (same shape as ui_state.edges).
structure.subgraphInputs: Array of exposed inputs: [{name, portType, paramType, innerNodeId, innerHandleId}].
structure.subgraphOutputs: Array of exposed outputs: same shape as subgraphInputs.

node_types.label: Subgraph Input
node_types.description: Input port of the parent subgraph.

node_types.label: Subgraph Output
node_types.description: Output port of the parent subgraph.

node_types.label: Prompt List
node_types.description: Ordered list of text prompts with weights.

node_types.label: Prompt Blend
node_types.description: Blend/interpolate between multiple prompt lists over time.

node_types.label: Scheduler
node_types.description: Time-based scheduler — fires values at given beats / times.

node_types.label: Output
node_types.description: Alternate sink node used by some UI graphs — prefer 'sink'.
